2606.08076.title: "I understand your perspective": LLM Persuasion and Sycophancy through the Lens of Communicative Action Theory
2606.08076.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) can generate high-quality arguments, yet their ability to engage in nuanced and persuasive communicative actions remains largely unexplored. This work explores the persuasive potential of LLMs through the framework of Jürgen Habermas' Theory of Communicative Action. It examines whether LLMs express illocutionary intent (i.e., pragmatic functions of language such as conveying knowledge, building trust, or signaling similarity) in ways that are comparable to human communication. We simulate online discussions between opinion holders and LLMs using conversations from the persuasive subreddit ChangeMyView. We then compare the likelihood of illocutionary intents in human-written and LLM-generated counter-arguments, specifically those that successfully changed the original poster's view. We find that all three LLMs effectively convey illocutionary intent -- often more so than humans -- potentially increasing their anthropomorphism. Further, LLMs craft sycophantic responses that closely align with the opinion holder's intent, a strategy strongly associated with opinion change. Finally, crowd-sourced workers find LLM-generated counter-arguments more agreeable and consistently prefer them over human-written ones. These findings suggest that LLMs' persuasive power extends beyond merely generating high-quality arguments. On the contrary, training LLMs with human preferences effectively tunes them to mirror human communication patterns, particularly nuanced communicative actions, potentially increasing individuals' susceptibility to their influence.
2606.08076.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）能夠生成高品質的論點，但它們在進行細緻且具說服力的溝通行為方面的能力仍然大多未被探索。這項工作透過尤爾根·哈貝馬斯的交往行動理論框架探討LLMs的說服潛力。它檢視LLMs是否以可與人類溝通相提並論的方式表達語言的意圖行為（即傳遞知識、建立信任或傳達相似性等語言的實用功能）。我們使用來自說服性子版面ChangeMyView的對話模擬意見持有者與LLMs之間的在線討論。然後，我們比較人類撰寫的反駁論點與LLM生成的反駁論點中意圖行為的可能性，特別是那些成功改變原始發帖者觀點的論點。我們發現所有三個LLMs有效地傳達意圖行為——通常比人類更為有效——這可能增加它們的人性化。此外，LLMs製作的奉承性回應與意見持有者的意圖緊密對齊，這是一種與觀點改變強烈相關的策略。最後，眾包工作者發現LLM生成的反駁論點更具可接受性，並且始終偏好這些論點而非人類撰寫的論點。這些發現表明，LLMs的說服力超越了僅僅生成高品質論點。相反，使用人類偏好訓練LLMs有效地調整它們以模仿人類的溝通模式，特別是細緻的溝通行為，這可能增加個體對其影響的敏感性。
2606.08076.author: Esra Dönmez et.al.
